---
title: "Bioethics: An Open Educational Resource"
subtitle: "Course Notes and Lecture Slides"
author: "Brendan Shea, PhD"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

## About This Course

This is an open educational resource (OER) for a course in **bioethics** — the study of ethical issues arising from advances in biology, medicine, and healthcare.

All materials are freely available for non-commercial educational use under a [Creative Commons BY-NC 4.0 license](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/4.0/).

---

## Course Materials

| # | Part | Topic | Slides | Notes |
|---|------|-------|--------|-------|
| 1A | Principles & Reasoning | The Four Principles; Reading a Moral Argument | [Slides](chapters/01-introduction-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/01-introduction.html) |
| 1B | Principles & Reasoning | Ethical Theories as Lenses | [Slides](chapters/01b-theories-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/01b-theories.html) |
| 2A | History & Relationship | From Hippocrates to the Belmont Report | [Slides](chapters/02a-history-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/02a-history.html) |
| 2B | History & Relationship | The Clinical Relationship: Consent, Autonomy, and Confidentiality | [Slides](chapters/02b-clinical-relationship-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/02b-clinical-relationship.html) |
| 3A | Research Ethics | How Clinical Research Works (and Why It's Different from Care) | [Slides](chapters/03a-clinical-research-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/03a-clinical-research.html) |
| 3B | Research Ethics | From Tuskegee to Today: Protecting Research Subjects | [Slides](chapters/03b-protecting-research-subjects-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/03b-protecting-research-subjects.html) |
| 4A | Reproductive Ethics | Abortion: Moral Status & Major Philosophical Arguments | [Slides](chapters/04a-abortion-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/04a-abortion.html) |
| 4B | Reproductive Ethics | Abortion: Law, Cases, and the Four Principles | [Slides](chapters/04b-abortion-law-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/04b-abortion-law.html) |
| 5A | Reproductive Ethics | Paternalism and Its Limits | [Slides](chapters/05a-paternalism-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/05a-paternalism.html) |
| 5B | Reproductive Ethics | Making Family: Markets, Surrogacy, and Parental Rights | [Slides](chapters/05b-reproductive-markets-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/05b-reproductive-markets.html) |
| 6A | Genomics & Enhancement | Reading Genomes: Prediction, Privacy, and Dual Use | [Slides](chapters/06a-genomics-reading-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/06a-genomics-reading.html) |
| 6B | Genomics & Enhancement | Engineering Persons: CRISPR, Enhancement, and Identity | [Slides](chapters/06b-genomics-enhancement-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/06b-genomics-enhancement.html) |
| 7A | End-of-Life Care | Death, Dying, and Advance Planning | [Slides](chapters/07a-death-dying-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/07a-death-dying.html) |
| 7B | End-of-Life Care | Euthanasia, Assisted Death, and Their Critics | [Slides](chapters/07b-euthanasia-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/07b-euthanasia.html) |
| 8A | Justice & Health Care | Theories of Justice and the Right to Health Care | [Slides](chapters/08a-justice-health-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/08a-justice-health.html) |
| 8B | Justice & Health Care | Why US Health Care Costs So Much — and Delivers So Little | [Slides](chapters/08b-us-healthcare-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/08b-us-healthcare.html) |
| 9A | Public Health Ethics | From Patient to Population | [Slides](chapters/09a-public-health-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/09a-public-health.html) |
| 9B | Public Health Ethics | False Beliefs and the Ethics of Correction | [Slides](chapters/09b-public-health-beliefs-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/09b-public-health-beliefs.html) |
| 10A | AI in Medicine | How Medical AI Works and Where It Goes Wrong | [Slides](chapters/10a-medical-ai-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/10a-medical-ai.html) |
| 10B | AI in Medicine | The Fifth Principle: Consent, Accountability, and Black Boxes | [Slides](chapters/10b-fifth-principle-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/10b-fifth-principle.html) |
| 11A | Gender, Identity & Disability | Gender-Affirming Care: Deciding Under Uncertainty | [Slides](chapters/11a-gender-affirming-care-slides.html) | [Notes](chapters/11a-gender-affirming-care.html) |

---

## Case Studies

We offer comprehensive historical and clinical case studies to pair with specific lecture topics. Each case is available as a web page or a downloadable Word document for offline reading, annotating, or printing.

| # | Case Study Title | Primary Paired Lecture | Formats |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Dax Cowart:** Refusing Burn Treatment and the Limits of Patient Autonomy | [L2B: Consent & Autonomy](chapters/02b-clinical-relationship.html) | [HTML](cases/01-dax-cowart.html) / [DOCX](cases/01-dax-cowart.docx) |
| 2 | **Tuskegee & Guatemala:** Deception, Racism, and Vulnerability in Research | [L3B: Protecting Subjects](chapters/03b-protecting-research-subjects.html) | [HTML](cases/02-tuskegee-guatemala.html) / [DOCX](cases/02-tuskegee-guatemala.docx) |
| 3 | **Sylvain Lesné & the Aβ*56 Affair:** Research Misconduct, Replication, and the Amyloid Hypothesis | [L3B: Protecting Subjects](chapters/03b-protecting-research-subjects.html) | [HTML](cases/03-alzheimers-fraud.html) / [DOCX](cases/03-alzheimers-fraud.docx) |
| 4 | **Kate Cox & Damla Karsan:** Conscientious Refusal, Conscientious Provision, and Professional Integrity Post-*Dobbs* | [L4B: Abortion Law](chapters/04b-abortion-law.html) | [HTML](cases/04-kate-cox-karsan.html) / [DOCX](cases/04-kate-cox-karsan.docx) |
| 5 | **Adriana Smith:** Brain Death, Pregnancy, and the Limits of Fetal-Protection Law | [L5A: Paternalism](chapters/05a-paternalism.html) | [HTML](cases/05-adriana-smith.html) / [DOCX](cases/05-adriana-smith.docx) |
| 6 | **He Jiankui & the CRISPR Twins:** Germline Editing, Consent, and the Limits of Scientific Self-Regulation | [L6B: Engineering Persons](chapters/06b-genomics-enhancement.html) | [HTML](cases/06-he-jiankui.html) / [DOCX](cases/06-he-jiankui.docx) |
| 7 | **Noelia Castillo:** Psychiatric Suffering, Decisional Capacity, and the Limits of Euthanasia Law | [L7B: Euthanasia & Assisted Death](chapters/07b-euthanasia.html) | [HTML](cases/07-noelia-castillo.html) / [DOCX](cases/07-noelia-castillo.docx) |
| 8 | **Singapore's Health Care System:** Cost, Access, Outcomes, and What the US Can (and Can't) Learn | [L8B: Why US Health Care Costs So Much](chapters/08b-us-healthcare.html) | [HTML](cases/08-singapore-healthcare.html) / [DOCX](cases/08-singapore-healthcare.docx) |
| 9 | **Andrew Wakefield & the MMR-Autism Myth:** Misinformation, Vaccine Hesitancy, and Public Trust | [L9B: False Beliefs & the Ethics of Correction](chapters/09b-public-health-beliefs.html) | [HTML](cases/09-wakefield-mmr.html) / [DOCX](cases/09-wakefield-mmr.docx) |
| 10 | **The Optum Algorithm:** Racial Bias, Proxy Variables, and Justice in Medical AI | [L10A: How Medical AI Works](chapters/10a-medical-ai.html) | [HTML](cases/10-optum-algorithm.html) / [DOCX](cases/10-optum-algorithm.docx) |


---

## How to Use These Materials

- **Slides** are built with [reveal.js](https://revealjs.com/) — press `F` for fullscreen, `S` for speaker notes, `?` for all shortcuts.
- **Notes** are the full HTML version with a table of contents.
- All source files (`.qmd`) are available on [GitHub](https://github.com/brendanpshea/bioethics-oer).

---

## License

[![CC BY-NC 4.0](https://licensebuttons.net/l/by-nc/4.0/88x31.png)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/4.0/)

This work is licensed under a [Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/4.0/).

You are free to share and adapt these materials for non-commercial purposes, provided you give appropriate credit.
